sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 36-year-old female, presents with a 2-day history of left ankle pain and swelling after slipping on ice and twisting her ankle on the outside of her hip.
She reports falling and being unable to walk due to pain and swelling.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation, with minimal relief.
She denies bleeding or pain in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She takes metformin and norvasc.
Her last hemoglobin A1c was 6.8, which is considered good.
She is due for an eye exam in the next couple of weeks.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Metformin [DOSE NOT MENTIONED] [FREQUENCY NOT MENTIONED] [INDICATION: diabetes]
Norvasc [DOSE NOT MENTIONED] [FREQUENCY NOT MENTIONED] [INDICATION: high blood pressure]
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: 120/70
Pulse rate: [NOT MENTIONED]
Oxygen saturation: [NOT MENTIONED]
Temperature: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
Neck exam is fine, no bruits.
Lungs are clear.
Heart exam is normal, with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
Belly exam is good.
Ankle exam reveals tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling and redness.
There is pain with valgus stressing of the ankle, but normal sensation and pulses.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has an ankle sprain, and an x-ray is ordered to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; diabetes; high blood pressure; suspected fracture (unknown).
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the ankle to rule out a fracture.
I will give the patient a splint and crutches to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I want the patient to keep the ankle elevated and apply ice.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer the patient to orthopedics.
I will also check the patient's hemoglobin A1c and order baseline labs.
I will refill the patient's norvasc prescription today.
Follow-up:
I will call the patient back if the x-ray shows a fracture.
The patient will follow up with the ophthalmologist for their eye exam in the next couple of weeks.
